title: Handlers, Middleware, Dependencies, and Application Architecture · Runtime Contract: Domain Transport Separation
summary: Handlers construct database pools and loggers per request, middleware changes response order, mutable globals leak across tests, and business rules cannot run without HTTP fixtures. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo domain transport separation increment in composed handler and dependency boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, serialization, and external effects so core rules are testable without HTTP.
Construct long-lived immutable dependencies at startup and inject narrow interfaces without service locators or per-request pool creation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Domain Transport Separation · Theory Domain Transport Separation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2110 in a civic incident API behind one trusted regional proxy. Build deterministic Go evidence to trace middleware order while keeping response ownership explicit; then cancel during the dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, serialization, and external effects so core rules are testable without HTTP.
